name: Laromestrocel improved 6-minute walk distance in aging frailty
summary: A 2026 phase 2b trial reported dose- and time-dependent physical-function improvement after allogeneic MSC infusion in older adults with aging frailty.
statement: In 148 older adults with aging frailty, laromestrocel infusion produced clinically meaningful dose- and time-dependent 6-minute walk test gains versus placebo, reaching 63.4 m at month 9 and 41.3 m at month 6.
population_or_model: Ambulatory adults aged 70 to 85 with aging frailty and hypomobility
time_horizon: Single infusion with 6- and 9-month functional follow-up
quantitative_note: The month 9 6MWT difference was 63.4 m (95% CI 17.1 to 109.6; p = 0.0077); the month 6 difference was 41.3 m (95% CI -2.4 to 84.9; p = 0.0635).
caveats: The strongest reported functional signal was at the later month 9 time point, while the month 6 confidence interval crossed zero.
Laromestrocel is generally interpreted as an allogeneic MSC or medicinal signaling cell product, so the mechanism may be paracrine or immunovascular rather than durable tissue stem-cell replacement.
The result needs independent replication and longer follow-up before it can support a durable aging-intervention claim.
